title: Fecal incontinence documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports relevant medical history, contributory factors, physical and emotional impact, general examination, anorectal examination only if performed, cognition, and warning context.
examination.rationale: The exact adult baseline section supports bowel symptoms, contributory factors, physical, emotional, social, cognitive, and functional impact plus clinician-performed examination only when entered.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports relevant medical history, contributory factors, physical and emotional impact, general examination, anorectal examination only if performed, cognition, and warning context.

source.title: Faecal incontinence in adults: management — Recommendations

investigations.text: Supports clinician-entered symptom review, prior management, specialist assessment status, preferences, dignity, and follow-up documentation.
investigations.rationale: The exact review section supports existing investigation context, prior management, preferences, dignity, specialist status, and clinician-entered follow-up without generating treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-entered symptom review, prior management, specialist assessment status, preferences, dignity, and follow-up documentation.

source_limitations: Episode pattern, stool consistency, obstetric or surgical history, consent, chaperone, examination, cognition, diagnosis, tests, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
